entry.title: Re: Instance creation for apiportal-apiaccess plan in btp...

Hi,

I'm facing the same issue.

I'm setting up Cloud ALM and currently working in the test tenant.

How did you manage to resolve this?

Thanks!

entry.title: How to create communication arrangement
entry.content:
We want to allow a non-SAP cloud application called Quickbase access to our S/4hana Private cloud system. Quickbase has given us a document titled "Set Up Authentication for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Extensions" to follow but this seems to apply to the Public cloud edition only. Could you provide guidance as to how we can create the same extension from our S/4Hana system? Is there a similar guide to follow that shows us how we can expose our API externally to quickbase?

SAP Note 3104502 said you can facilitate communication with OData services by exposing OData ICF services using standard on-premise processes. However, I don't know how to go about this. I need the OData services exposed externally.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to create communication arrangement
entry.content:
Exposing OData services requires providing access to the OData service URL through your company firewall. You can use the SAP Web Dispatcher or another network routing product to provide this access at the network level.

For authentication, the third-party service will need to log in using whatever authentication method you have configured. See SAP Gateway Authentication and Single Sign-On

entry.title: Re: Is there a BTP service or API to track user login/logout times and session duration?
entry.content: Is there just someone who can help me?

entry.title: New Data Architecture in SAP FI on S/4HANA Cloud: What Every Functional Consultant Needs to Know
entry.content:
What is the Universal Journal (ACDOCA)?

The Universal Journal is one of the most significant changes in SAP S/4HANA, revolutionizing how financial and accounting data are stored and processed. Instead of relying on multiple tables for different areas, the ACDOCA table unifies data from various modules into a single structure, providing a simplified and much more analytical data model.

The ACDOCA table includes data from:

  • Financial Accounting (FI)
  • Controlling (CO)
  • Asset Accounting (AA)
  • Materials Management (MM)
  • Sales and Distribution (SD)
  • Profitability Analysis (CO-PA)

As a result, SAP offers a harmonized, simplified, and highly analytical architecture, making the data model more accessible and agile, while also improving performance and increasing integration across modules.

Key Operational Benefits

  1. Elimination of Manual Reconciliations: Centralizing data in the ACDOCA table removes the need to perform checks and reconciliations across multiple tables. As a single source of truth, it simplifies complex processes and increases data reliability.
  2. Faster Report and KPI Generation: Data is now accessed centrally, allowing for faster and more efficient retrieval. Integration with analytics tools like SAP Analytics becomes smoother, enabling real-time management reports and KPIs.
  3. Technical Simplification: Older tables like BSEG, COEP, BSIS, and others have been replaced by views accessible via CDS. This means that functionalities depending on these tables will still work but will need revision to adapt to the new architecture. Less complexity leads to fewer errors and easier maintenance.
  4. Improved Performance: In Cloud scenarios, SAP HANA In-Memory makes data processing significantly faster. The ACDOCA table, indexed for high performance, ensures quick queries and real-time responses, a major leap forward, especially for businesses dealing with large volumes of data.

What Does "Indexed Table" Mean?

An indexed table is a database table that has an index associated with it. The index is a data structure that facilitates fast retrieval of specific values within a table, thereby improving query performance. In traditional databases, when you run a query (such as a SELECT), the system needs to scan the entire table to find records that match the specified criteria. If the table is large, this can be a time-consuming process.

By creating an index, the database generates an additional search structure that stores search keys (e.g., values from a column) in an ordered form. This allows the database to quickly locate the desired data without scanning the entire table.

For example, if you have a customer table with millions of records and want to search for information based on the ID (a unique column), you can create an index on the ID column. When you run the query, the database can use the index to quickly find the desired ID without having to go through the entire table.

Practical Example of a Query

Previously, to query transactions for a cost center, you would need to join multiple tables in ECC, such as BSEG, COEP, and BSIS. 

 
SELECT belnr, bukrs, gjahr, kostl, hkont, wrbtr, shkzg, zuonr
FROM bseg
JOIN coep ON bseg.belnr = coep.belnr
JOIN bsis ON bseg.belnr = bsis.belnr
WHERE coep.kostl = '1000' AND bseg.gjahr = '2025';

In S/4HANA, with ACDOCA, a single SELECT solves the whole process, without the need for multiple complex joins.

SELECT belnr, bukrs, gjahr, kostl, hkont, wrbtr, shkzg, zuonr
FROM acdoca
WHERE kostl = '1000' AND gjahr = '2025';

As you can see, the new model eliminates the need for joins across several tables, making the process simpler and faster.

Classic Tables vs. ACDOCA in SAP S/4HANA

Functionality / Process

Classic Tables (ERP ECC)

Replacement in S/4HANA

Journal Entries

BSEG

ACDOCA

CO Documents

COEP, COBK

ACDOCA

General Ledger Balances

BSIS (open), BSAS (cleared)

ACDOCA with item status

Accounts Receivable (Customers)

BSID, BSAD

ACDOCA

Accounts Payable (Vendors)

BSIK, BSAK

ACDOCA

Asset Accounting

ANEP, ANEA, ANLP, ANLC, ANEK

ACDOCA

Material Accounting

MLIT, MLPP, MLCR, MLHD, etc.

ACDOCA (if integrated)

Profitability Analysis (CO-PA)

CE1xxxx, CE2xxxx, CE3xxxx

ACDOCA (with CO-PA Universal)

Overhead Costs (CO)

COSP, COSS

ACDOCA + analytical views

Additional Resources

To gain a deeper understanding of the changes in SAP S/4HANA Cloud and how you can adapt your processes to this new data structure, check out the following SAP KBAs:

  • KBA 3321191: How to maintain Database Hints for optimizing CDS views for program “Release from Universal Journals” in S/4HANA Finance for Group Reporting
  • KBA 3345584: How to extend Display Line items in General Ledger (ACDOCA) with BSEG

These links are essential for understanding all the technical details of the new data architecture and how to make the most of the simplified Universal Journal model.

Final Tip

Mastering the concept of the Universal Journal and the ACDOCA table is not just a technical skill, but a strategic competence. Professionals who understand and master this architecture will be better positioned to add value to transformation projects and process optimization in SAP S/4HANA Cloud. If you are a SAP FI functional consultant transitioning to S/4HANA, investing in the understanding of ACDOCA is crucial for your journey.

The future of accounting and finance in SAP is already here. Prepare to be part of this transformation.

#UniversalJournal #ACDOCA

entry.title: Deletion of Texts using BAPI_WRF_LAYMOD_SAVEREPLICA
entry.content:
Hello experts

In BAPI_WRF_LAYMOD_SAVEREPLICA, I wanted to delete layout module texts; there is a requirement that texts in certain languages are deleted. If I specify the elements that I want to delete in the interface DESCRIPTIONS with the deletion indicator FUNCTION = 003 and specify the corresponding layout module LAYOUT_MODULE with the deletion indicator FUNCTION = 004, it does not work.

Is there anything to consider when deleting sub-elements of a layout module?

Thank you very much for your advice and support

Best regards
Kurt

entry.title: Re: Ariba CLID Integration to SAP S/4 Hana On-prem
entry.content: Do you have any documentation related to that?

entry.title: Re: Professional services. Employee time with Sales price 0.
entry.content:
Hello @kat_kos01 

Thanks for sharing the screenshots , they help confirm your scenario.

From the “Manage Prices - Sales” app, you’ve correctly set up the project-specific sales price for the employee using condition table 040 (Customer Project & Employee & WBS Element), but in the billing simulation, the system still applies a billing rate equal to the cost rate (0) instead of the expected sales price.

This behaviour occurs when SAP cannot resolve the pricing condition record during billing  usually due to

  • Mismatch in key fields like WBS element or personnel number
  • Missing/incorrect condition record validity
  • Sales price not released (approval status still “In preparation”)
  • Wrong Pricing Procedure or pricing condition type not picked up in the simulation

Recommendation

  • Confirm WBS element is passed correctly in billing simulation


In the billing screen, open the detailed pricing view and check

  • Which condition type is being triggered?
  • Which key fields (WBS, resource, org, etc.) are filled?
  • If condition type is PSPS (Project-Specific Price for Services), but no record found → system falls back to cost.
  • Check approval status in “Manage Prices – Sales”
    Ensure your pricing entry is not just “In Preparation”. Click “Release” if available.
  • Confirm key combination used matches what’s available in the sales document
    • If the project or WBS element is incorrect or at a higher/lower level than expected, the condition record will not be found.
    • Use “Display Billing Items” > Price Analysis to trace where the price is breaking.
  • Ensure no pricing condition suppression or alternative logic

Some customers define condition exclusion for 0-rated items. Confirm this isn’t active.

Next

  • Use the “Price Analysis” feature during billing simulation to confirm if condition record is found or skipped.
  • If the WBS or project is not resolving the price, try adding a broader pricing condition (e.g., just Personnel No. & Project) as a test.

SAP References 

  • KBA 2912341 – Condition record not picked in billing
  • KBA 2876814 – Non-billable behavior in billing and rev rec

With kind regards

Chuma

entry.title: Success Factors to AD integration question
entry.content:
We are enabling the SAP Success factors to AD integration using the standard pattern of HR driven provisioning. 

LINK

We have recently merged multiple domains into one and now several users have an existing employee ID which does not  match their employee ID from Success Factors, we want to ensure the employee number from Success factors is Primary but at the same time not create new Entries in AD for existing users when this employee number does not match between Entra and SF. 

As per the documentation - 
if personIdExternal maps to employeeId and is set as the matching attribute, then the provisioning service uses the personIdExternal value to search for the user with employeeId filter.
If a user match is found, then it updates the target attributes.
If no match is found, then it creates a new entry in the target.

I wanted to know if we can have multiple checks before a new ID is created in AD/Entra ie check for personIDExternal and if not found, then check with firstName and lastName and if not found, then create a new entry else update. 

Any recommendations here would be very helpful, thank you.

entry.title: Re: Customer Vendor Integration..for Ariba

entry.title: Re: Adding SAP Cloud ALM to "Instances & Subscriptions" i...
entry.content:
Hi, Berkay_Ö,
As Dolores was suggesting, you are NOT in the right BTP global account.  You need to switch to other global account for Global/Subaccount of Cloud ALM.  I am attaching a screen from a Globa Account for SAP Cloud ALM:

and here is subaccount for Cloud ALM and I have highlighted the icon for Services > Instances and Subscriptions:

Subscriptions showing SAP Cloud ALM:

and the SAP Cloud ALM API instance for the service key/binding:


I hope this is helpful.

Best regards,
Paula

entry.title: Re: Meetup SAP 2025 no Rio de Janeiro!
entry.content: Que legal!!!! Bora lá!!!

entry.title: Re: Material stock must be booked out in last two MM periods
entry.content:
Hello, 

The message you checked on the screenshot is just information message that only warns you about quantity of your stock. It shouldn't block you to post the document. The following message is error about quantity on unrestricted stock that you can use, that isn't enought (lack of 9EA), that is why you can't post it. You might have some stock for example on quality or blocked type of stock. Please check on what type of stock you have this material. 

Regards, 

Jagoda

entry.title: Re: How to create additional depreciation areas in public...
entry.content:
@Fabio_Rizzi 

KBA https://me.sap.com/notes/0003530183 has the scenarios for ledgers that SAP recommend.

entry.title: How to stop Sending RFQ by Email to Vendor Automatically
entry.content:
Hi everyone! Can someone please tell me where in SAP S4 HANA could i stop the sending of aoutomatic RFQ emils to venderos.

Heres the thing, I dont need vendors to know about the purchase process, because we already know the prices and venders politics, and so those emails were not being sent, but some how now are, and I need to stop at least the process of sending RFQ.

entry.title: Re: Sap Public Cloud - auto population of the Confirmatio...
entry.content: Hello, could you clarify, you want to have this one CCK on every PO that you create in the system?

entry.title: End-to-End Maintenance Execution Using SAP Fiori Apps in SAP S/4HANA – A Phase-Wise Approach
entry.content:
Introduction:
In modern asset-intensive industries, the reliability and availability of technical assets directly impact business performance. SAP Plant Maintenance (SAP PM) in S/4HANA, integrated with SAP Fiori, transforms the traditional maintenance execution process into a user-centric, phase-wise digital journey.

This blog outlines the End-to-End Maintenance Execution Cycle using standard SAP Fiori apps, structured by maintenance phases – from initiation to completion.

Maintenance Execution Phases & SAP Fiori Apps:

Below is a breakdown of the 9 maintenance phases, each mapped with relevant SAP Fiori applications used in SAP S/4HANA:

1. Initiation Phase – Raise Maintenance Request

  • App:

    • Create Maintenance Request (F1511A)
      https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/#/detail/Apps('F1511A')/S30PCE

      Fiori ID: F1511A

    • My Maintenance Requests (F4513)
      https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/#/detail/Apps('F4513')/S30PCE

      Fiori ID: F4513

  • Business Role: Technician / Operator

  • Purpose: Report asset breakdown, malfunction, or maintenance need.

2. Screening Phase – Request Evaluation

  • App:

    • Screen Maintenance Request (F4072)
      https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/#/detail/Apps('F4072')/S30PCE

      Fiori ID: F4072 

  • Business Role: Maintenance Supervisor

  • Purpose: Validate, prioritize, and convert valid requests into maintenance notifications.

3. Planning Phase – Notification Planning

  • App:

    • Find Maintenance Notification (F2071)
      https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/#/detail/Apps('F2071')/S30PCE

      Fiori ID: F2071

  • Business Role: Maintenance Planner

  • Purpose: Plan operations, materials, required components, work centers, and document scope.

4. Approval Phase – Workflow Approval

  • App:

    • My Inbox (F0862)
      https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/#/detail/Apps('F0862')/S26OP

      Fiori ID: F0862

       

       

  • Business Role: Department Manager / Approver

  • Purpose: Approve maintenance plans based on cost, scope, and operational needs.

5. Preparation Phase – Capacity Planning

  • Apps:

    • Manage Maintenance Planning Buckets (F3888)
      https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/#/detail/Apps('F3888')/S30PCE

      Fiori ID: F3888

    • Manage Maintenance Buckets (F4073)
      https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/#/detail/Apps('F4073')/S30PCE 

      Fiori ID: F4073

       

  • Business Role: Maintenance Planner

  • Purpose: Review capacity, allocate resources into planning buckets.

6. Scheduling Phase – Order Scheduling

  • App:

    • Find Maintenance Order (F2175)
      https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/#/detail/Apps('F2175')/S30PCE

      Fiori ID: F2175

       

  • Business Role: Scheduler

  • Purpose: Schedule maintenance orders against available capacity and resource windows.

7. Execution Phase – Perform Maintenance

  • App:

    • Perform Maintenance Jobs (F5104A)
      https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/#/detail/Apps('F5104A')/S30PCE

      Fiori ID: F5104A

       

  • Business Role: Maintenance Technician

  • Purpose: Execute job cards, record time confirmations, consumption, and failure causes.

8. Post-Execution Phase – Technical Completion

  • App:

    • Find Maintenance Order (Set to TECO)
      https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/#/detail/Apps('F2175')/S30PCE

      Fiori ID: F2175

       

  • Business Role: Maintenance Supervisor

  • Purpose: Confirm job completion, close technical operations.

9. Completion Phase – Business Closure

  • App:

    • Find Maintenance Order (Set to CLSD)
      https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/#/detail/Apps('F2175')/S30PCE

      Fiori ID: F2175

       

  • Business Role: Maintenance Planner / Controller

  • Purpose: Final financial settlement, order closure, cost analysis.


Business Benefits of Fiori-Based Maintenance Phases:

  • Role-based access & intuitive UI

  • Improved maintenance planning & execution accuracy

  • Faster approvals via integrated workflow

  • Real-time visibility into asset status

  • Seamless collaboration between maintenance teams

Conclusion:
SAP S/4HANA with Fiori delivers an intelligent, mobile-ready, and user-friendly maintenance experience. By aligning the maintenance lifecycle with structured Fiori apps, organizations can reduce asset downtime, streamline approvals, and achieve operational excellence.

entry.title: HCM Brasil - Crédito do Trabalhador (eConsignado) - Considera 13º salário e aviso prévio
entry.content:
Olá pessoal, 

No dia 16 de junho de 2025, foi entregue uma atualização na solução do Crédito do Trabalhador para incluir diferenças de 13º salário e períodos de aviso prévio no cálculo da rubrica Parc/créd Rem. disponível (MCTN). 

A solução está disponível através da SAP Note 3615865 - BRPID - Adjustment in available remuneration to consider Christmas bonus differences and pre-notice period. 

As seguintes rubricas foram adicionadas para compor as deduções: 

  • /303 - Trib.INSS (13o) 
  • /403 - Tributo IRRF (13o) 
  • /374 - Aviso prévio Dedução 

Além disso, foram incluídas na base, rubricas que tenham selecionada pelo menos uma das seguintes classes de acumulação: 

  • 23 (Base de INSS para décimo-terceiro salário) 
  • 29 (Base de INSS para décimo-terceiro salário - outro emprego) 
  • 77 (Base de INSS para aviso prévio - 13º salário) 

Importante: As rubrica /303 é customizada na tabela T7BRSS. É possível customizar uma rubrica específica com o tipo C13INSS - Contribuição 13º INSS. 

Gostou desse post? Dê um Like e compartilhe o conteúdo com seus colegas. 

Acesse também nosso workzone exclusivo dedicado para informações sobre o Crédito do Trabalhador.   

Fiquem à vontade para deixar um feedback, comentário ou pergunta sobre este Blog post no espaço abaixo. E não esqueçam de seguir a tag HCM Payroll Brazil na SAP Community para ficarem ligados nas últimas notícias. 

Um abraço, 

Juliano Klein

entry.title: Re: Deprecation of Rehire Inactive Employee (1H 2025)
entry.content: Hi , Can we have a flag in the onboarding process to check if the candidate is a former employee ?

entry.title: Re: the billing staus of sales order item in the CDS
entry.content: Supporting the discussion, I still question the logic of how a sales order can be considered 'Completed' without invoicing. The most critical point is that the Overall Status changes to 'Completed' at the time of shipment creation, even before the Goods Issue (PGI). From a process perspective, the inventory has not been moved and the revenue has not been realized, but the system already marks the order as completed. This logic is inconsistent with the Order-to-Cash cycle. As a consequence, we, consultants, are forced to develop 'magic' and complex workarounds to define the real and reliable status of a sales order. Having to bypass such a basic standard functionality is, in fact, surreal and adds unnecessary complexity to our work.

entry.title: Re: What exactly is SAP BW PCE… and what it is not !
entry.content: Awesome blog @FrankRiesner ! Great overview on BW modernization in BDC and the different private cloud deployment options.

Wonderful blog!!

entry.title: Enhancing warehouse operations by integrating robotics with SAP Extended Warehouse Management
entry.content:
Introduction

Warehouse automation has been steadily gaining momentum, with robotic automation taking center stage in recent years. It is estimated that robot shipments to increase by up to 50 percent each year through 2030, with warehouse automation growing by more than 10 percent a year. The evolution from custom-built robots to modular and scalable platforms has marked a significant shift in the industry. This transformation is the result of advances in cloud technology, affordability of hardware for data storage and processing, significant improvements in sensor and computer vision technology, and more recently AI. Additionally, the availability of integration mechanisms and standards has enabled warehouse of all sizes to adopt robotic automation. While there is a wide choice of robot vendors, a robust and scalable warehouse management system is needed at the heart of the warehouse automation strategy.

In this blog series we explore how SAP S/4HANA Extended Warehouse Management (SAP EWM) enables robotic automation via seamless orchestration and task assignments while providing real-time visibility into the robotic automated processes.

Business and Operations Drivers for Robotic Automation

 Warehousing plays a critical role in the logistics orchestration of any supply chain when it comes to order fulfillment. Companies must juggle multiple factors to maintain timely deliveries to their customers such as

  • Keeping pace with order volumes while meeting customer service expectations of shorter lead times and order accuracy
  • Handling increasing number of stock-keeping-units (SKUs)
  • Addressing labor shortages while looking for ways to increase workforce productivity and worker safety
  • Managing operating costs associated with handling and storage
  • Ensuring accurate inventory
  • Maximizing space utilization

Specific industry needs layer additional factors:

  • Since COVID, e-commerce and retail industries went through a massive transformation. Companies around the globe have had to deal with massive increases in order volumes needing fast omnichannel fulfillment that require accurate picking in their warehouses and distribution centers, which in some countries has led to the evolution of quick commerce.
  • The manufacturing industry has the need to precisely handle materials and components and synchronize warehouse operations with production, for example managing Just-in-Time supply to the production lines, and intralogistics operations.
  • The third-party logistics companies who serve multiple clients need the ability to run their warehouses efficiently with a high degree of accuracy to be able to handle large volumes of products that flow through their facilities.

Lastly, disruptions caused by ongoing geopolitical tensions, extreme weather events, port congestions, maritime route disturbances, and trade wars continue to pressure companies to build greater resilience. As a result, to strengthen their end-to-end logistics process, companies are increasingly adopting robotics in their warehouse operations as an added dimension of automation to boost speed, throughput, and accuracy. Robots like high density storage and retrieval systems (ASRS), goods-to-person robots, collaborative robots (cobots) that work safely alongside humans, shuttle systems and AMRs perform functions that span a wide range of scenarios, providing several benefits to warehouse operations such as:

  • Increased Efficiency: Robots can handle repetitive tasks such as picking, packing, and sorting faster and more accurately than humans. This leads to quicker fulfillment of orders and improved throughput.
  • Reduced Labor Costs: By automating manual tasks, warehouses can reduce their dependence on human labor, which can lower costs like hiring and training.
  • Enhanced Accuracy: Robots equipped with advanced sensors and algorithms can minimize errors in tasks such as inventory management, ensuring higher accuracy in order fulfillment and reducing the return rate.
  • Consistent Performance: Robots can work continuous shifts without fatigue, maintaining consistent performance over time and increasing the reliability of operations.
  • Safety Improvement: Automating heavy lifting and other potentially dangerous tasks can reduce the risk of workplace injuries and accidents, ensuring a safer environment for human workers.
  • Scalability and Flexibility: Robotics systems can be scaled up or down depending on demand. They can also be reprogrammed for different tasks, providing flexibility in operations and facilitating adaptation to changing business needs.
  • Optimized Space Utilization: Robots can navigate tight spaces and difficult layouts, making it possible to optimize warehouse layout and storage, thereby maximizing the use of available space.
  • 24/7 Operation: Unlike human workers, robots can operate around the clock without breaks, enabling continuous operations and faster processing times.
  • Data Collection and Analysis: Warehouse robots can gather valuable data on operations, which can be analyzed to improve efficiencies, forecast inventory needs, and optimize warehouse management strategies.
  • Reduction in Human Error: Automation reduces the likelihood of human errors in processes such as inventory counts and order placement, enhancing overall accuracy.

Robotic Automation with SAP EWM

Before implementing robotic automation, companies should carefully evaluate their specific use cases to ensure that the technology aligns with their business needs and objectives. The use cases typically fall into the following areas:

  • loading / unloading,
  • put-away,
  • robotic / collaborative picking,
  • sorting,
  • packing,
  • palletizing / depalletizing,
  • replenishing.

And there is a high likelihood that as the use of robots proliferates in the warehouses, there will be a heterogeneous landscape of robot vendors making integration into the warehouse management systems very complex. To orchestrate seamless warehouse operations, a robust warehouse management system is central to the system landscape. It should be agnostic to various robotic types and vendors, providing the necessary APIs and business objects to enable efficient integration.

With customers in 24 industries across 75 industries, SAP EWM has been serving the warehouse management needs for decades. SAP EWM provides comprehensive automation capabilities for various warehouse processes.  SAP’s approach to robotic automation with SAP EWM is built upon these core capabilities and providing customers a choice of specialized partners with certified integration to SAP EWM as illustrated below.

Source: SAP EWM Product Management (Note: the partners listed are not an exhausted list)

These partners fall into two categories and work in concert with SAP EWM:

  • OEM Fleet Management System that integrates and manages robots from different manufacturers within a single system
  • Multiagent Orchestration Platform, a term coined by Gartner. These platforms act like intelligent middleware that integrates and orchestrates work between various business applications, heterogeneous fleets of operational robots, and other automated agents like doors or elevators. These solutions orchestrate and assign work and monitor and coordinate the activities of diverse fleets of robots.

Partner solutions for both categories can be found on SAP Store:

Partner

Solution

Description

Locus Robotics, Inc.

Locus Autonomous Mobile Robots for SAP EWM

Innovative autonomous mobile robots integrate with SAP Extended Warehouse Management to work collaboratively alongside workers in fast-paced logistics and fulfillment industries. Workers can pick 2-3 times faster with near 100% accuracy and less labor, delivering higher productivity.

Geekplus Technology Co., Ltd.

Improve efficiency of your warehouse operation by 2 to 3 times

Innovative autonomous mobile robots integrate with SAP Extended Warehouse Management to work collaboratively alongside workers in fast-paced logistics and fulfillment industries. Workers can pick 2-3 times faster with near 100% accuracy and less labor, delivering higher productivity.

DXC Technology Services LLC

DXC Smart Warehouse EWM Integration with AGVs

The solution, built on SAP BTP, seamlessly integrates SAP Extended Warehouse Management (EWM) with advanced automation technologies such as robotic picking systems, Automated Guided Vehicles (AGVs), and sophisticated conveyor belts. This integration greatly enhances operational efficiency in warehouses by streamlining processes, accelerating dispatch times, reducing the likelihood of human errors, and cutting labor costs.

Flexus AG

AGV-Hub - Fleet Manager for Mobile Robots

One fleet manager to control your whole fleet of mobile robots: traffic management, battery management, evacuations, idle management, and an overview of all errors. Monitor all vehicles with or without the VDA5050 and connect them seamlessly into your SAP systems like SAP EWM, SAP Production Planning or SAP Stock Room Management.

KINEXON Industries GmbH

Connect and automate operations: redefine manufacturing & logistics efficiency

Elevate productivity and sustainability: Track and trace all moving assets in real-time and orchestrate all mobile robots (AMR/AGV) in production and intralogistics. Enhance production speed, quality, scalability, and efficiency. Build a digital twin and automate at scale. Seamlessly integrate with SAP Digital Manufacturing and SAP Extended Warehouse Management for operational synergy and E2E process management.

Roboteon Inc.

Roboteon Fulfillment Platform

Plug into and optimize existing automated warehouse distribution operations running on SAP EWM. Plan, simulate and deploy greenfield automation projects. Design automation strategies in a mixed SAP landscape: part or fully automated, or fully manual operations looking to automate. Leverage IDOC or API based integration on SAP BTP. We also support older SAP EWM releases moving to SAP Extended Warehouse Management, industry add-on for SAP S/4HANA.

To efficiently integrate warehouse robotics and fleet management systems to EWM on SAP S/4HANA, we are continuously working to enhance availability, capability and extensibility and providing the necessary APIs. You can find these APIs for EWM via the SAP Business Accelerator Hub by entering “warehouse” in the search bar.

Benefits of Warehouse Robotic Automation with SAP EWM

 SAP’s approach provides several benefits to customers as they begin to adopt and / or expand the use of robots in their warehouses and fulfillment centers, all in a flexible manner. Standard connectivity and certified integration, including keeping pace with evolving industry standards, helps companies develop their automation strategies in all types of environments – fully manual, partially automated, as well as fully-automated. Working with a choice of partners, both the multi-agent orchestration platforms as well as OEM fleet management system providers, customers can plug into an existing or a greenfield automation environment running on SAP EWM and plan, optimize, simulate and deploy robots to enable collaborative and efficient warehouse task orchestration. The biggest benefit is that customers are not locked into a particular robot vendor, there is no proprietary dependence on any robotics solution. This approach provides the ability to integrate with multiple robot vendors and instances across their facilities allowing customers to choose the best robot solution for the process they are looking to automate.

 Conclusion

 The future of warehouse automation depends on seamless integration between SAP EWM and robotics and fleet management systems, where businesses that leverage this integration will gain a competitive edge in efficiency, accuracy, and scalability. In the future blogs in this series, we will explore some of the options SAP provides through our partner ecosystem that integrate with SAP EWM and help unlock the full potential of warehouse robotics.

entry.title: Create Unique Label for Class Description Field
entry.content:
We would like to use the Description field in a class to provide participants with the online meeting link. Currently, this field is labeled generically as "Description," which can lead to confusion about its purpose. By relabeling it to "Online Meeting Link", we can clearly communicate its intended use, improving the learner experience and reducing ambiguity.

Currently, the label label.Description is shared between both the item and class description fields. By separating these and assigning a distinct label to the class description, administrators can customize the label to reflect its specific use case—such as displaying the online meeting link.

If you also have this need, please vote for the enhancement request 350149 Improvement Request Details - Customer Influence

entry.title: Re: Cycle Counting Error , Material found: old stock=0
entry.content: You can then post the difference right, what is the issue in that?

entry.title: Re: "No stock posting posting possible for this material"
entry.content:
Hello Asad, 

 

Please revisit the OMS2 transaction. Enter your material type, and then on the left side, select "Quantity/Value Updation" from the dialog structure. Choose your Valuation Area (i.e., your Plant) and material type. You will see a checkmark under both Quantity Updation and Valuation Updation. Please keep the checkmark under Quantity Updation, but remove the checkmark under Valuation Updation. Don’t forget to save your changes, then save and exit. After that, proceed to MIGO.

 

thanks,
Ankur

entry.title: Re: Cycle Counting Error , Material found: old stock=0
entry.content: Hi Ankurch,

entry.title: Re: Cycle Counting Error , Material found: old stock=0
entry.content: i have sufficent stock in mmbe and LS24 but while cyclic count the system shows error like Material found : old stock=0

entry.title: Re: Depreciation areas 92 and 93 not available for Sweden...

Hello, 
I saw you had an incident created for this topic, the local product manager, the functionality delivered is sufficient to meet the SE legal requirements.
Kind regards
Mark

entry.title: Re: ABAP RAP managed scenario Default values
entry.content:
How does your determination definition look like? Do you use "on save"?

determination onSave on save { create; }

https://help.sap.com/doc/abapdocu_cp_index_htm/CLOUD/en-US/abenbdl_determinations.html 
Have you already tried "on modify"?

determination onCreate on modify { create; }

entry.title: Re: How to use ABAP Test Cockpit (ATC) in the cloud as a developer in an on-premise system
entry.content:
Hi @skl .

does this happen as you execute ATC run from BTP over on-premise system or from on-premise system using the check variant of ATC on BTP? Are all prerequisites in on-premise system fulfilled (see the collective SAP Note 2364916 under the "Checked System" section)? Which check causes the problem?

Kind Regards,
Olga.

entry.title: Re: How to work with exemptions using ABAP Test Cockpit (ATC) in the cloud during development in an
entry.content:
Hi @WandersonSantos ,

for approving/rejecting/deleting exemptions your user must be assigned a business role based on the template SAP_BR_QUALITY_MNGR_SW_DEV (Quality Manager – Software Development).

Kind regards,
Olga.

entry.title: Re: ABAP Test Cockpit in the Cloud – How to use baseline
entry.content:
Hi @YogeshKothavade ,

the baseline is effective as long as the related code sections remain unchanged: if the code is changed or a new code is added, the unique identifier for each ATC finding changes so that it does not match any more with the finding that has been added to the baseline. Therefore you get the findings again if you add new source code to your program.

Kind Regards,
Olga.

entry.title: Re: Is it possible to Update Simulation End Macro
entry.content:
Hi Nagaraj,

Yes, it is possible to change that text.  The text is located in the Standard Playback Dictionary.  Start editing the Standard Playback Dictionary that is available in the Resources > Playback Dictionaries section in Producer.  Then modify the text in the following entry:

After modifying the entry, finish editing the Standard Playback Dictionary object and publish it.

Regards
Shane

entry.title: Re: Document type field modification (BSART) in the purch...
entry.content:
Hi Marco,

SAP did not provided document type change option for Service PO and FO due to technical reasons, pls check the KBA - 

634395 - FAQ: Purchase orders in the service

 

Thanks,
Ankur

entry.title: Re: SAP SBO SDK SAPbouiCOM.WebBrowser - SAP 10 FP2111
entry.content:
It is really sad that this question was never answered.

While the Windows client is transitioning to a maintenance state a lot of stuff can still be done with the embedded browser, while the web client catches on.

And the solution is really quite simple. This little snippet is everything you need to open a specific business partner, for example:

<a href="#" onclick="window.external.b1link('OCRD', 'CL-00001'); return false;">Open BP master data for CL-00001</a>

 Apply the same principle to any other type of window.

The only related documentation I found is from 2011 (14 years ago now), and you can find it here: How to Develop Your Own Dashboards for SAP Business One

I hope this helps some future readers, because the original poster is by now listed as a "former member"...

Cheers!

Joerg.

entry.title: Re: Cycle Counting Error , Material found: old stock=0
entry.content: can you share the detailed screenshot of error here?

entry.title: Re: SAP SBO SDK SAPbouiCOM.WebBrowser - SAP 10 FP2111
entry.content: The SAPbouiCOM.WebBrowser has a way to communicate back with the application. The SDK documentation includes this object that should provide the necessary information to work on the SDK side. However, there is no documentation I could find that shows how to actually send the message from the browser. Can you help there? See: https://help.sap.com/doc/089315d8d0f8475a9fc84fb919b501a3/10.0/en-US/SDKHelp/SAPbouiCOM~SBOWebMessageEventArg.html

entry.title: Re: SAP FIORI - Upload General Journal Entries
entry.content:
Not necessarily the app is not active.

A very common reason for such error is the user not having access to such app. Ideally those 2 should be in the same catalog so anyone how can access one of them will be able to call the other one.

entry.title: JOB EC-LMS keeps failing after maintenance
Professional Service : Copy Project with Staffed Resource
entry.content:
Hi,
Contexte
In SAP Public Cloud, for a professional Services Project, we create our project in copy of a project template with use the Fiori app "Plan Customer Projects". These templates are used as the starting point for new projects, and contain staffed resources for the different WBS of the project.

Issue
Currently, when we use the "Copy project", and select "Work Packages, Services, and Resources", the resources are not being copied. We are looking into the feasibility of copying resources in this public cloud project. Anyone has seen this before? 
We activated the scope 6J0 and also found this OS Note: 2700550 , however configuration point is not available.
Thank you.

entry.title: Re: SAP Signavio: How to Convert yyyy-mm-ddThh:mm:ss (Str...
entry.content: <delete>

entry.title: Re: SAP Signavio: How to Convert yyyy-mm-ddThh:mm:ss (String) to yyyy-mm-dd hh:mm:ss (Timestamp)
entry.content:
Hi @bene4

 

Thank you for your response. I tried the SQL you provided, but the same error appears.

When I executed only the Replace statement on "yyyy-mm-ddThh:mm:ss", it was successfully converted to "yyyy-mm-dd hh:mm:ss". Therefore, based on the error message, I'm wondering if there's an issue with the "yyyy-mm-dd hh:mm:ss" format. I also tried specifying "YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS" (uppercase), but the same error occurred. I think I'll do some more research.

Best regards,

Maki

SAP Signavio Process Intelligence

entry.title: Re: Power of Attorney vs. Payment Approvers: Key Differen...
entry.content: No, if we change in the approvers at bank account level, the Power of Attorney functionality will not be affected.

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA TM– Conversational planning using Generative AI!! step by step configuration walkthr
entry.content:
@ZenonK : its yet to be available under https://www.sap.com/products/artificial-intelligence/use-cases.html

In case you need demo, reach out to SAP accounting team or SAP Premium Engagement team at your customer. They will be able to arrange a demo.

entry.title: Re: How to stop Sending RFQ by Email to Vendor Automatically
entry.content:
Hi! If you want to stop automatic RFQ emails from being sent to vendors in SAP S/4HANA, you can check these things first:

  1. Check the Output Determination for RFQ in SPRO:
    Go to SPRO → MM → Purchasing → Messages → Request for Quotation → Output Determination
    Look for message type AEN or similar and either deactivate the condition record or set output type to ‘NEU – Print Only’ instead of email.
  2. Check if Email Output is Enabled in NACE or BRF+, depending on whether your system is using classic or BRF+ output management.
  3. Check Vendor Master (XK03 / BP) - see if an email ID is maintained under communication. If yes, and output is active, system might auto-send.

 

Just to clarify is your system using BRF+ for output control, or classic NACE? That will decide where exactly you need to deactivate it

entry.title: Re: SOAP API: Journal Entry – Clearing (Asynchronous) did...
entry.content:
Hi Chuma

Thanks for the reply.

I know this is an asynchronous process, but when I checked the invoices in the SAP system (which were used in this request) after some time, I found that their status was still not cleared. So I suspect that this clear operation has not been executed successfully, that's why I need to find the execution result log.

entry.title: Insurance Manual Task Creation - Activity Management
entry.content:
Hi SAP Experts,

We’re currently configuring manual task creation within the SAP Insurance Claims Management module in ECC, and I’d appreciate your help!

We would like to define a fixed list of manual tasks for claim handlers to select from via the dropdown in the “Create Manual Task” pop-up in the claim overview screen.

So far, I have:

  1. Created the task via IMG path:
    Claims Management > Activity Management > Define Tasks, Log Entries, and Activities

  2. Assigned the task to the correct internal claim type version via:
    Claims Management > Claim > Define Internal Claim Types > Permitted Manual Tasks/Log Entries

Despite this, when I try to create a manual task from a claim, the task dropdown is empty.

Are there any additional configuration steps, authorizations, or missing dependencies (e.g., activity categories, language entries, processing types, etc.) I should check?

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Cloud ALMのBW向けのセットアップガイドと、ガイドに記載が有る適用必須なNoteの差異について
entry.content:
下記ガイド見てCloud ALMのBW向けセットアップをしています。
https://support.sap.com/en/alm/sap-cloud-alm/operations/expert-portal/setup-managed-services/setup-abap-740.html?anchorId=section_406552075_co_80551802

セクション「Technical Prerequisites」に 3530899
の適用が必要と記載が有りますが、Noteの中身を見ると「This note is only relevant for ERP systems. 」との記載が有り、ガイドとNoteに差異があります。

Cloud ALMを利用するうえで必要なBWシステムでの設定として、DIGITSの適用は不必要という理解で正しいでしょうか？



------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Cloud ALMのBW向けのセットアップガイドと、ガイドに記載が有る適用必須なNoteの差異について
entry.content:
はい、ご認識の通りです。
ERP/S4HANAタイプのABAPシステムのみBusiness Process Monitoring（BPM）がサポートいます。
BWシステムにて、Business Process Monitoring に関するノートを適用する必要はございません。

ZFiori.

entry.title: Re: Enabling SAP Build Process Automation's My Inbox App in SAP Build Work Zone, Standard Edition
entry.content: @Gunnar_L This is happening to use across two difference client accounts at the moment, I feel something is going on.

entry.title: Re: Inside ABAP Conf 2025: Innovation, Insight, and the Power of Community
entry.content:
Thank you for your feedback and encouragement @Jelena_Perfiljeva 

The in person events are great and getting them locally is difficult way down here at the bottom of Australia in Melbourne.  But we do run our own community events and I had some good discussions with fellow SAP community location group member, @walid, to discuss how events are done in different places and think of creative ways we can crank them up. So in that regard it was inspiring too. But getting the chance to mix with like minded people in different regions really helps open up to new ideas and confer on the similarities too.

I am also keen to catch up on all the talks I missed from the other sites. Vienna was a particularly glam location, nice work 🙂

entry.title: Re: Como agregar nuevos campos a variante del Contenedor...

Irene

entry.title: Re: 交易性金融资产需要当客户/供应商管理，同时需要指定现金流
entry.content:
Hi Yanxi,

以下APP或者API均含有“金融交易类型”字段，可能在不同APP的中文描述不一致，但字段是同一个，具体信息也可以参考附件。

1. 创建进项发票APP：银行现金科目的行项目明细界面中点击“更多”后弹出的对话框中的“事务类型”字段即为“金融交易类型”字段。

2. 过账付款和付款清账APP：银行现金科目的行项目明细行展开后可以找到“金融交易类型”字段。

3. API：不确定你提到的API具体是哪个，但以标准API Journal Entry - Post (Synchronous) 为例，可以参考链接https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/b978f98fc5884ff2aeb10c8fdeb8a43b/2c0ccd5780726264e10000000a4450e5.html?state=DRAFT 找到“金融交易类型”字段。

Kind Regards,

Lynette Sun

entry.title: Re: 物料主数据中的销售文本如何批量维护？
entry.content: Hi @Ping, to share this valuable information with other customers, I have translated you solution in English and Accept this as Solution on behalf of you. "Mass creation for Material master data can be done vie Data Migration approach".

entry.title: Re: Introducing the SAP Cloud SDK for AI (JavaScript/TypeScript) 🎉
entry.content:
Hi Community，

Can we try to add and really support this model "anthropic--claude-3.7-sonnet"? It will works with new version of Go very well .

New versions of Go (like Go 1.21 or 1.22) introduce syntax or semantics changes which gpt-4o can not recognize very well. So we want move to the model "claude".

Even the DOC here https://github.wdf.sap.corp/pages/AI/generative-ai-hub-sdk/_reference/README_sphynx.html  show our community support this "anthropic--claude-3.7-sonnet", but in  SDK of @SAP-ai-sdk/foundation-models, I don't think it really support it now.

entry.title: Re: 统驭科目记账屏幕放出利润中心/自定义字段
entry.content:
Hello @YANXI ,

  1. 对于客商维度（对应统驭科目），无法直接添加利润中心字段。建议通过凭证分割派生的方式实现客商的利润中心确定(Document Splitting Characteristic for General Ledgers)
  2. 激活利润中心的凭证分割后，在对方科目行（如收入/费用科目）过账时填入利润中心，对应利润中心可以被派生至AR/AP中
  3. 通过API过账时同理，利润中心填入对方科目行

Kind Regards,

entry.title: Re: CCode XYZX: Fiscal year change 2025 has failed for some fixed assets (Message number AU271) SAP
entry.content: Hi Rahman, may I know did you get the solution? I am facing the same problem. Thank you!

entry.title: Delegatee is not able to complete the tasks
entry.content: When a task is assigned to a Delegatee , not able to see the tasks, Blank screen opens up. All permissions are in place.

entry.title: Re: Interview Scheduling Outlook
entry.content:

When Outlook Integration is done, only the company domain emails availability will be shown .  Please check the email associated with the RCMADMIN. 

Hope this helps.

Thanks

Anamika

entry.title: Re: SAC Planning with connection to ERP System

entry.title: Re: Can we do value mapping through MDG Consolidation ?
entry.content:
Hello T,

Yes, we have specific adapters to use for different scenarios. Adapter - 'CL_MDC_ADAPTER_BP_VALUEMAP_STD' is if you would like to consider value mapping. 

Please find below link for more details on the available adapters. 

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/6d52de87aa0d4fb6a90924720a5b0549/a3bd115449e7bb29e10000000a44538d.html

Regards,
Saravanan

entry.title: Re: I am having issue when trying to initiate Service Ins...

Seems there is no standard API. Is there a way to have custom API to meet the requirement.

entry.title: Re: Mass Maintenance Product: No process template for pro...
entry.content:
Hi Josef,

Please check below points.

1. Verify if the required Business functions and services are activated for consolidation and mass processing in the system. 

Configuration of MDG, Consolidation and Mass Processing | SAP Help Portal

2. Verify if the required authorizations are assigned to the user. 

Authorization Objects Used by MDG, Consolidation and Mass Processing | SAP Help Portal

Regards,
Saravanan

entry.title: Re: Incorrect delivery status "complete" after partial de...
entry.content: Remove the tick against Relevant for Delivery since you are trying to change the existing configuration and system will not allow such text item categories to set this box.  Once it is saved successfully, either with an existing TR or new TR, come out of the screen and again now try to remove the Item Type with NEW transport request.  If this is a production issue, you can move this new TR to PRD

entry.title: Why my read in behavior implementation only called when edit
entry.content: i still confused with the logic of read in unmanaged behavior, why my read not called when i click go in list report and view the object page

entry.title: Re: Free SAP Learning Hub, explore student Edition
entry.content:
Hello and thank you very much.

I have the Student Edition but after numerous failed attempts to access Practice System, I was informed that  Practice System does NOT come with the Student Edition. It saddens me because I'm new to SAP and I learn through practical hands-on activities. Contrary to, "Cloud-based practice systems are included for hands-on experience", I am wishfully hoping that I was given the wrong information, inadventently.

If indeed cloud-based Practice Systems are included with the Student Edition, please lead me to the water -- lol.

Thank you in advancce!

entry.title: Re: Netting in Accounts Receivable and Accounts Payable in SAP S/4HANA
entry.content:
@rakhmatilla_tuganov wrote:

In this blog post, I would like to cover a small yet useful functionality in the Accounts Payable (AP) module of SAP S/4HANA: Netting of Accounts Receivable (AR) and Accounts Payable (AP). While researching this topic online, I found limited end-to-end documentation, so this blog is aimed at filling that gap with a clear walkthrough.

Scope of the Blog

  • Create a Business Partner and extend it to Supplier (FI) and Customer (FI) roles

  • Create AR and AP invoices

  • Execute netting using the Automatic Payment Program (APP)

1. Create a Business Partner and Extend It to Customer and Supplier Roles

First, create a Business Partner (BP) who will act as both a supplier and a customer. To do this, use the "Maintain Business Partner" app.

  • Select "Organization" as the BP type.

  • In the "Business Partner (Gen.)" role, go to the "Payment Transactions" tab and maintain the Bank Details (Bank Key, Account Number, IBAN, etc.) as shown in the screenshot below:

Next, extend the same BP to the following roles:

  • Customer (FI)

  • Supplier (FI)

Within the Company Code section of each role, ensure the following:

  • In the Payment Transactions tab, specify a Payment Method that will later be used in the APP.

  • Tick the "Clearing with Vendor"/"Clearing with Customer" checkbox. This setting is critical for enabling netting between AR and AP.

Finally, under the Supplier (FI) role, make sure to fill in the Customer Number in the General Data section to establish the link between the customer and supplier accounts:

2. Create AR and AP Invoices

Now, create AR and AP invoices using the customer and supplier accounts created in the previous step. A few important points to remember:

  • Leave the Payment Method field empty in both documents. If it is filled, the APP will treat them as separate payments, defeating the purpose of netting.

  • Ensure that the total payable amount is greater than the receivable, so that the APP will generate a net payment for the difference.

This ensures that the system can offset the AR and AP balances during payment processing.

3. Schedule the Automatic Payment Program (APP)

Use the "Manage Automatic Payments" app to execute the netting process:

  • Create a new parameter run and enter the relevant Customer/Supplier number, Company Code, and Payment Method.

  • Ensure the invoices are due on the payment run date.

  • Run the Payment Proposal and check the results.

If everything is configured correctly, you will see a single payment that offsets both open items and pays only the balance.

Conclusion

This simple configuration allows you to reduce unnecessary cash flows and administrative efforts by settling AR and AP balances for the same business partner via netting. Although small, this functionality can be very useful in real-world business scenarios—especially when dealing with intercompany transactions or recurring vendors who are also customers.

Regards,
Rakhmatilla Tuganov

Hello @rakhmatilla_tuganov,
This blog fills a critical documentation gap by providing a clear step-by-step guide on netting AR and AP in SAP S/4HANA. The walkthrough ensures smooth execution, from setting up a Business Partner to processing invoices efficiently. Well-structured and highly useful! kroger feedback



Best Regards,
James Batson

entry.title: Re: SSAM2310 Previous meter reading not fetching to Mobil...
entry.content:
Hi please check your exchange framework filter, by standard sap has some exclude filter enabled, disable that. Also have a close look on your exchange log table /*/*eabl.

Also check filters and data distribution read filters of your omdo id *meter*reading.

entry.title: MQC value not copied to material list post batch determination
entry.content:
Hello All,

As per formula in master recipe, value is calculated but batch value is not adjusted and copied to material list.

Please guide me on this issue.

Regards,

Priya
